title: Trema blancoi
taxonomy: Trema blancoi is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cannabaceae. It is a member of the genus Trema and is native to tropical Asia. The Cannabaceae family is a group of flowering plants that includes several species of trees and shrubs. The genus Trema is part of the larger clade known as Cannabaceae, which is a family of flowering plants that are commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. intro: Trema blancoi, also known as Blanco's trema or white trema, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cannabaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Trema blancoi include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit potential health benefits. The most common herbal preparations made with Trema blancoi include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma.
